003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.junit5;
018
019import java.util.function.*;
020
021import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
022
023/**
024 * Fluent {@link BasicBeanStore} subclass for declaring test-time bean overrides.
025 *
026 * <p>
027 * A {@code TestBeanStore} is an overlay-builder.  Tests register replacements for production beans
028 * via {@link #override} and then plug the resulting store into the SUT &mdash; typically through a
029 * builder hook such as {@code MockRestClient.Builder.overridingBeanStore(...)} (the Mode INJECT wiring
030 * pattern; see {@link JuneauBeanStoreExtension} for the full contract).  The framework threads the
031 * overlay into the {@code overridingParent} slot of the SUT's bean store, putting registered overrides
032 * at tier 1 of the resolution chain (above local resource entries and the Spring bridge).
033 *
034 * <p>
035 * <b>No JUnit 5 dependency.</b>  This class is intentionally a plain {@link BasicBeanStore} subclass
036 * so that callers from non-JUnit harnesses (TestNG, raw {@code main(...)}, Spring Test, etc.) can
037 * use it without dragging in {@code junit-jupiter-api}.
038 *
039 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
040 * <p class='bjava'>
041 *    <jc>// Build the overlay.</jc>
042 *    <jk>var</jk> <jv>overlay</jv> = <jk>new</jk> TestBeanStore()
043 *       .override(MyExternalApi.<jk>class</jk>, mockApi)
044 *       .override(CallLogger.<jk>class</jk>, () -&gt; spyLogger);
045 *
046 *    <jc>// Wire into the SUT.</jc>
047 *    <jk>try</jk> (<jv>client</jv> = MockRestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>(MyResource.<jk>class</jk>)
048 *          .overridingBeanStore(<jv>overlay</jv>)
049 *          .build()) {
050 *       <jv>client</jv>.get(<js>"/widgets/1"</js>).run().assertStatus().is(200);
051 *    }
052 * </p>
053 *
054 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
055 *    <li class='jc'>{@link BasicBeanStore} - The bean store this overlay extends.
056 *    <li class='jc'>{@link StackOverlay} - The stack-aware composition primitive used for class-scope and Mode OVERLAY layering.
057 *    <li class='jc'>{@link JuneauBeanStoreExtension} - Mode INJECT vs Mode OVERLAY contracts and the JUnit 5 lifecycle owner.
058 * </ul>
059 *
060 * @since 10.0.0
061 */
062@SuppressWarnings({
063   "resource" // Fluent override(...) methods return this (a Closeable BeanStore) owned by the caller/SUT; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
064})
065public class TestBeanStore extends BasicBeanStore {
066
067   /**
068    * Constructor.
069    */
070   public TestBeanStore() {
071      super();
072   }
073
074   /**
075    * Parent-chaining constructor.
076    *
077    * <p>
078    * Lookups consult this store's local entries first and then fall through to the supplied parent.  Used by
079    * {@link JuneauBeanStoreExtension} to stack a per-method overlay on top of a per-class overlay so method-scope
080    * overrides shadow class-scope overrides of the same {@code (type, name)} pair.
081    *
082    * @param parent The parent bean store.  Can be <jk>null</jk> for a standalone overlay.
083    * @since 10.0.0
084    */
085   public TestBeanStore(BeanStore parent) {
086      super(parent);
087   }
088
089   /**
090    * Registers an unnamed bean as a test-time override.
091    *
092    * @param <T> The bean type.
093    * @param type The bean type.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
094    * @param bean The override instance.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
095    * @return This object.
096    */
097   public <T> TestBeanStore override(Class<T> type, T bean) {
098      addBean(type, bean);
099      return this;
100   }
101
102   /**
103    * Registers a named bean as a test-time override.
104    *
105    * @param <T> The bean type.
106    * @param type The bean type.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
107    * @param bean The override instance.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
108    * @param name The bean name.  Can be <jk>null</jk> for unnamed beans.
109    * @return This object.
110    */
111   public <T> TestBeanStore override(Class<T> type, T bean, String name) {
112      addBean(type, bean, name);
113      return this;
114   }
115
116   /**
117    * Registers an unnamed supplier-backed bean as a test-time override.
118    *
119    * <p>
120    * The supplier is invoked lazily on first lookup, mirroring {@link BasicBeanStore#addSupplier}.
121    *
122    * @param <T> The bean type.
123    * @param type The bean type.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
124    * @param supplier The override supplier.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
125    * @return This object.
126    */
127   public <T> TestBeanStore override(Class<T> type, Supplier<T> supplier) {
128      addSupplier(type, supplier);
129      return this;
130   }
131
132   /**
133    * Registers a named supplier-backed bean as a test-time override.
134    *
135    * <p>
136    * The supplier is invoked lazily on first lookup, mirroring {@link BasicBeanStore#addSupplier}.
137    *
138    * @param <T> The bean type.
139    * @param type The bean type.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
140    * @param supplier The override supplier.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
141    * @param name The bean name.  Can be <jk>null</jk> for unnamed beans.
142    * @return This object.
143    */
144   public <T> TestBeanStore override(Class<T> type, Supplier<T> supplier, String name) {
145      addSupplier(type, supplier, name);
146      return this;
147   }
148}
